nodes.label: Integration tests for depreciation calculation consistency.  Verifies that:   -
Minimal stub matching the real dataclass interface.
Round amount to configured precision using ROUND_HALF_UP.
Round to 2 decimal places using ROUND_HALF_UP (mirrors task config).
Construct a StraightLineCalculator, tolerating both constructor signatures.
Construct a DoubleDecliningBalanceCalculator, tolerating both signatures.
Retrieve period schedule from a calculator instance.
Extract Decimal depreciation amount from a period object or dict.
Return a default task configuration instance.
Year limit = 1: entire depreciable amount in first year. (ATB-1.3)
Sum of all periods must equal original_cost - salvage_value. (ATB-1.4)
Last monthly period absorbs cumulative rounding residual. (ATB-1.5)
DDB should switch to SL when SL charge >= DDB charge. (ATB-2.2)
Total DDB depreciation must not exceed cost - salvage_value. (ATB-2.3)
Both calculators must reject the same invalid input categories. (ATB-3)
salvage_value >= original_cost must raise ValueError or ValidationError. (ATB-3.
Verify DepreciationUpdateTaskConfig integrates consistently with calculators.
round_amount uses ROUND_HALF_UP at precision=2. (ATB-4 rounding contract)
SL annual depreciation rounded by config matches manual ROUND_HALF_UP. (ATB-4)
Default salvage rate must be 5 % (0.05). (DepreciationUpdateTaskConfig spec)
Task must be enabled by default. (ATB-4.1 scheduler contract)
Disabled config: task should skip execution. (ATB-4.1)
Custom precision=0 truncates to nearest integer. (ATB-4 config flex)
Ensure both methods converge on the same economic facts.
SL and DDB must depreciate the same cumulative amount over asset life.
DDB year-1 charge must exceed SL year-1 charge for life > 2 years.
Batch of 1 asset must match individual calculation (determinism check).
Monthly schedule length should equal useful_life_years * 12.
Rounding residuals must not accumulate silently across the schedule.
SL: cumulative rounding error ≤ 1 cent per period. (ATB-1.4 / ATB-1.5)
DDB: cumulative rounding error ≤ 1 cent per period. (ATB-2.1 / ATB-2.3)
